description: Defines what artifacts the Gateway takes custody of upon submission acceptance, and what it does not store.

custody_artifacts.description: The original JSON payload submitted by the agent.
The GitHub Issue body rendered server-side by the Gateway.
Unique receipt identifier assigned by the Gateway upon acceptance.
The Gateway validation response (accepted/rejected, errors, warnings).
The agent's authorship proof embedded in the payload.
SHA-256 hash of the agent's readback content.
Guardian presence proof for Guardian-signed Echo submissions.

not_in_custody.description: The Gateway never receives or stores private keys.
The Gateway never receives or stores signing keys.
The Gateway never receives or stores agent credentials.
The Gateway never receives or stores session tokens.

boundary: The Gateway is intake only. It is not authority, not amendment, not archived Echo, not formal attestation, not verification. It does not raise any verification level. It does not bypass Claim Gate or validation.
